question: [File: 'src/interfaces/ICallbacks.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: IRepayCallback.onRepay / repay] Can an unprivileged borrower-as-callback-receiver, authorized for a lender, under preconditions where repay() has already decremented position[id][onBehalf].debt and incremented marketState[id].withdrawable before onRepay fires, trigger the sequence repay(units, onBehalf, callback, data) -> onRepay calls withdraw(market, units, lender, attacker), violating the invariant that loan tokens must be received before withdrawable credit can be redeemed, causing scoped impact: attacker drains existing withdrawable loan token balance from the protocol before the repay transferFrom executes, effectively obtaining a zero-cost flash loan from the protocol's existing lender pool? Proof idea: fuzz units in [1, lender.credit], assert protocol loanToken balance never decreases net-negative across the full repay call, and assert withdrawable[id] == withdrawable_before + units - units == withdrawable_before after the full tx.

[File: 'src/interfaces/ICallbacks.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: ILiquidateCallback.onLiquidate / liquidate] Can an unprivileged liquidator-callback under preconditions where liquidate() has already incremented marketState[id].withdrawable by repaidUnits and transferred seizedAssets collateral to receiver before onLiquidate fires, trigger the sequence liquidate(seizedAssets, repaidUnits, borrower, receiver=callback, callback=callback) -> onLiquidate calls withdraw(market, repaidUnits, lender, callback), violating the invariant that loan tokens must be received before withdrawable credit can be redeemed, causing scoped impact: callback receives repaidUnits of loan tokens from existing protocol balance before paying repaidUnits back, netting seized collateral for free if the protocol has sufficient pre-existing withdrawable balance? Proof idea: set up market with large existing withdrawable from prior repayments, fuzz repaidUnits, assert protocol loanToken balance is non-negative at every intermediate step and that the liquidator's net token gain equals exactly seizedAssets * price / lif.

[File: 'src/interfaces/ICallbacks.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: IBuyCallback.onBuy / take] Can an unprivileged taker-as-buyerCallback under preconditions where take() has already updated buyerPos.credit, sellerPos.debt, consumed[maker][group], totalUnits, and claimableSettlementFee before onBuy fires but before any token transfer, trigger the sequence take(buyOffer, units, taker, receiver, buyerCallback=attacker) -> onBuy calls take(same offer, units2, taker, receiver, buyerCallback=attacker) recursively, violating the invariant that consumed[maker][group] must not exceed offer.maxAssets/maxUnits across all fills, causing scoped impact: offer group budget exceeded because the outer take's consumed increment is visible to the nested take's check, allowing double-fill of the same offer group in one transaction? Proof idea: fuzz units and units2 such that units + units2 > offer.maxUnits, assert consumed[maker][group] <= offer.maxUnits after both takes complete.

[File: 'src/interfaces/ICallbacks.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: ISellCallback.onSell / take] Can an unprivileged seller-as-callback under preconditions where take() has set the liquidation lock via tExchange(LIQUIDATION_LOCK_SLOT, id, seller, true) and sellerPos.debt has been incremented, trigger the sequence take(buyOffer, units, taker, receiver, sellerCallback=attacker) -> onSell calls repay(market, units2, seller, address(0), '') to reduce seller.debt before the health check at line 476, violating the invariant that the health check must reflect the seller's true debt obligation from the take, causing scoped impact: seller borrows units of loan tokens while only maintaining health via a same-transaction repayment that is not guaranteed to persist, allowing net undercollateralized debt creation if the repay is funded by flash-borrowed assets? Proof idea: assert position[id][seller].debt after the full take equals debt_before + sellerDebtIncrease - units2, and assert isHealthy holds with the final debt value.

[File: 'src/interfaces/ICallbacks.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: ISellCallback.onSell / take] Can an unprivileged seller-as-callback under preconditions where the seller has debt and the liquidation lock is set, trigger the sequence take(buyOffer, units, taker, receiver, sellerCallback=attacker) -> onSell calls withdrawCollateral(market, collateralIndex, assets, seller, receiver) to reduce collateral before the health check at line 476, violating the invariant that collateral cannot be withdrawn if it would make debt unhealthy, causing scoped impact: seller withdraws collateral during the sell callback window, and the health check at the end of take uses the reduced collateral, potentially allowing the tx to revert but leaving a window where collateral was transiently removed? Proof idea: assert isHealthy(market, id, seller) holds with the post-withdrawal collateral at the time of the health check at line 476.

[File: 'src/interfaces/ICallbacks.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: multicall / take + withdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged borrower under preconditions where the borrower has sufficient collateral to pass health after borrowing, trigger the multicall sequence [take(sellOffer, units, borrower, ...), withdrawCollateral(market, collateralIndex, assets, borrower, receiver)] in one transaction, violating the invariant that collateral cannot be withdrawn if it would make debt unhealthy, causing scoped impact: borrower obtains loan tokens and withdraws collateral in the same tx, leaving a net undercollateralized position if the health check in withdrawCollateral uses stale oracle prices or rounding that differs from the take's health check? Proof idea: fuzz units and assets near the LLTV boundary, assert isHealthy(market, id, borrower) after both calls complete.

[File: 'src/interfaces/ICallbacks.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: multicall / repay + withdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged borrower under preconditions where the borrower has debt and collateral, trigger the multicall sequence [repay(market, units, borrower, address(0), ''), withdrawCollateral(market, collateralIndex, assets, borrower, receiver)] in one transaction, violating the invariant that collateral cannot be withdrawn if it would make debt unhealthy, causing scoped impact: borrower repays partial debt and immediately withdraws collateral that was previously locked, with the health check in withdrawCollateral using the post-repay debt, potentially allowing more collateral withdrawal than would be safe if the repay is later reversed? Proof idea: assert position[id][borrower].debt * WAD <= sum(collateral[i] * price[i] * lltv[i]) after both calls.

[File: 'src/interfaces/ICallbacks.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: IRepayCallback.onRepay / repay + withdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged borrower-as-callback under preconditions where repay() has decremented position[id][onBehalf].debt before onRepay fires, trigger the sequence repay(market, units, borrower, callback, data) -> onRepay calls withdrawCollateral(market, collateralIndex, assets, borrower, receiver), violating the invariant that collateral withdrawal health check must use the debt that will persist after the full transaction, causing scoped impact: borrower withdraws collateral based on the reduced debt during the callback window, but if the repay's transferFrom subsequently fails (e.g., insufficient allowance), the entire tx reverts including the collateral withdrawal, but the window reveals whether the check is correctly ordered? Proof idea: assert that withdrawCollateral inside onRepay uses the same debt value as after the full repay completes.

[File: 'src/interfaces/ICallbacks.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: IFlashLoanCallback.onFlashLoan / flashLoan + take] Can an unprivileged taker under preconditions where the taker has no loan tokens but has collateral, trigger the sequence flashLoan([loanToken], [buyerAssets], callback, data) -> onFlashLoan calls take(sellOffer, units, taker, receiver, takerCallback=address(0)) using flash-loaned tokens as the payer, then repays the flash loan from the sellerAssets received, violating the invariant that the taker must have genuine capital to fund a borrow, causing scoped impact: taker creates debt position with zero upfront capital by using the protocol's own flash loan to fund the take, with the net effect that the taker has debt but the loan tokens came from the protocol's own balance? Proof idea: assert protocol loanToken balance is non-negative after the full flashLoan call and that position[id][taker].debt == units.

[File: 'src/interfaces/ICallbacks.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: IFlashLoanCallback.onFlashLoan / flashLoan + liquidate + withdraw] Can an unprivileged liquidator under preconditions where the protocol has existing withdrawable loan tokens from prior repayments, trigger the sequence flashLoan([loanToken], [repaidUnits], callback, data) -> onFlashLoan calls liquidate(market, collateralIndex, 0, repaidUnits, borrower, false, callback, callback, data2) -> onLiquidate calls withdraw(market, repaidUnits, lender, callback), then repays flash loan from withdrawn tokens, violating the invariant that the liquidator must provide net new capital equal to repaidUnits, causing scoped impact: liquidator seizes collateral while recycling the protocol's own withdrawable balance to fund the repayment, netting seized collateral for free? Proof idea: assert liquidator's net loanToken outflow equals repaidUnits and net collateral inflow equals seizedAssets, with no protocol balance decrease.

[File: 'src/interfaces/ICallbacks.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: multicall / liquidate + withdraw] Can an unprivileged liquidator under preconditions where a borrower is liquidatable and lenders have credit, trigger the multicall sequence [liquidate(market, collateralIndex, 0, repaidUnits, borrower, false, receiver, address(0), ''), withdraw(market, repaidUnits, lender, receiver)] in one transaction, violating the invariant that withdrawable must be backed by actual loan token deposits, causing scoped impact: lender withdraws the repaid units in the same tx as the liquidation, racing with other lenders to claim the newly available withdrawable balance, potentially causing a DoS for other lenders if the withdrawable is fully drained? Proof idea: fuzz repaidUnits and assert sum of all lender withdrawals <= marketState[id].withdrawable at all times.

[File: 'src/interfaces/ICallbacks.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: ILiquidateCallback.onLiquidate / liquidate + liquidate] Can an unprivileged liquidator-as-callback under preconditions where a borrower has multiple collaterals and is liquidatable, trigger the sequence liquidate(market, collateralIndex0, 0, repaidUnits0, borrower, false, receiver, callback, data) -> onLiquidate calls liquidate(market, collateralIndex1, 0, repaidUnits1, borrower, false, receiver, address(0), '') again on the same borrower, violating the invariant that bad debt must be realized exactly once per liquidation event and that the loss factor update must be applied atomically, causing scoped impact: double bad debt realization corrupts lossFactor and totalUnits, causing lenders to lose more credit than the actual bad debt? Proof idea: assert marketState[id].lossFactor after both liquidations equals the value computed by applying both bad debt amounts sequentially, and assert sum of lender credit decreases equals total bad debt.

[File: 'src/interfaces/ICallbacks.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: multicall / liquidate + liquidate (same borrower, different collaterals)] Can an unprivileged liquidator under preconditions where a borrower has two activated collaterals and is unhealthy, trigger the multicall sequence [liquidate(market, 0, 0, repaidUnits0, borrower, false, receiver, address(0), ''), liquidate(market, 1, 0, repaidUnits1, borrower, false, receiver, address(0), '')] in one transaction, violating the invariant that the RCF (recovery close factor) must be evaluated against the borrower's state after the first liquidation, causing scoped impact: second liquidation uses stale maxDebt computed before the first liquidation's collateral seizure, potentially allowing over-liquidation beyond what the RCF permits? Proof idea: assert that after both liquidations, position[id][borrower].debt >= maxDebt_after_first_liquidation - epsilon for the RCF threshold.

[File: 'src/interfaces/ICallbacks.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: IBuyCallback.onBuy / take (enterGate bypass)] Can an unprivileged taker under preconditions where the market has an enterGate that blocks canIncreaseCredit for the buyer, trigger the sequence take(buyOffer, units, buyer, receiver, buyerCallback=attacker) -> onBuy calls setIsAuthorized(attacker, true, buyer) then calls take(buyOffer2, units2, buyer, receiver, buyerCallback=address(0)) where the gate check for buyer's credit increase is evaluated after the authorization change, violating the invariant that enterGate.canIncreaseCredit must be checked for every credit increase, causing scoped impact: buyer increases credit in a gated market without passing the gate check by composing authorization and take in the same callback? Proof idea: assert that every path through take that results in buyerCreditIncrease > 0 calls IEnterGate(enterGate).canIncreaseCredit(buyer) and receives true.

[File: 'src/interfaces/ICallbacks.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: multicall / setIsAuthorized + take (enterGate bypass)] Can an unprivileged attacker under preconditions where the market has an enterGate that checks isAuthorized state, trigger the multicall sequence [setIsAuthorized(attacker, true, victim), take(offer, units, victim, receiver, takerCallback=address(0))] in one transaction where the gate's canIncreaseDebt reads isAuthorized state, violating the invariant that gate checks must reflect the authorization state at the time of the take, causing scoped impact: attacker grants themselves authorization and immediately uses it to increase victim's debt in a gated market within the same transaction? Proof idea: assert that isAuthorized[victim][attacker] was false before the multicall and that the gate check cannot be bypassed by same-tx authorization changes.

[File: 'src/interfaces/ICallbacks.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: ISellCallback.onSell / take (liquidatorGate bypass via callback)] Can an unprivileged liquidator under preconditions where the market has a liquidatorGate that blocks the liquidator, trigger the sequence take(buyOffer, units, taker, receiver, sellerCallback=attacker) -> onSell calls liquidate(market, collateralIndex, 0, repaidUnits, borrower, false, receiver, address(0), '') where the liquidatorGate check uses msg.sender which is the Midnight contract during the callback, violating the invariant that liquidatorGate.canLiquidate(msg.sender) must check the original liquidator's address, causing scoped impact: blocked liquidator bypasses the liquidatorGate by routing the liquidation through a sell callback where msg.sender is the callback contract? Proof idea: assert that in all liquidate calls, msg.sender is the original external caller and not an intermediate callback contract.

[File: 'src/interfaces/ICallbacks.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: IFlashLoanCallback.onFlashLoan / flashLoan (liquidatorGate bypass)] Can an unprivileged liquidator under preconditions where the market has a liquidatorGate that blocks the liquidator's address, trigger the sequence flashLoan([loanToken], [repaidUnits], callback, data) -> onFlashLoan calls liquidate(market, collateralIndex, 0, repaidUnits, borrower, false, receiver, address(0), '') where msg.sender is the callback contract (not the blocked liquidator), violating the invariant that liquidatorGate.canLiquidate must check the original transaction initiator, causing scoped impact: blocked liquidator bypasses the liquidatorGate by routing the liquidation through a flash loan callback? Proof idea: deploy a liquidatorGate that blocks address X, have X call flashLoan with a callback that calls liquidate, assert the liquidation reverts.

[File: 'src/interfaces/ICallbacks.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: multicall / take (sell) + take (sell) (double borrow)] Can an unprivileged borrower under preconditions where the borrower has sufficient collateral to pass health after each individual borrow, trigger the multicall sequence [take(sellOffer1, units1, borrower, ...), take(sellOffer2, units2, borrower, ...)] in one transaction, violating the invariant that the health check after each take must use the cumulative debt from all prior takes in the same transaction, causing scoped impact: borrower creates more debt than their collateral supports because each take's health check only sees the debt from that single take, not the combined debt from both takes? Proof idea: assert isHealthy(market, id, borrower) after both takes complete using the sum of units1 + units2 as the total debt.

[File: 'src/interfaces/ICallbacks.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: ISellCallback.onSell / take (nested sell, debt accumulation)] Can an unprivileged seller-as-callback under preconditions where the seller has collateral and the offer has sufficient maxUnits, trigger the sequence take(buyOffer, units1, taker, receiver, sellerCallback=attacker) -> onSell calls take(buyOffer, units2, seller, receiver, sellerCallback=attacker) recursively, violating the invariant that the health check at the end of the outer take must reflect the total debt from both the outer and inner takes, causing scoped impact: seller accumulates debt from both takes but the outer take's health check at line 476 only sees the final state after the inner take's health check already passed, potentially allowing the outer take to pass health with a position that would fail if evaluated at the outer take's debt level? Proof idea: assert position[id][seller].debt after both takes equals units1 + units2 and isHealthy holds for that total debt.

[File: 'src/interfaces/ICallbacks.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: IBuyCallback.onBuy / take (state observation before token transfer)] Can an unprivileged buyer-as-callback under preconditions where the buyer's credit has been increased and consumed[maker][group] has been updated before onBuy fires, trigger the sequence take(buyOffer, units, buyer, receiver, buyerCallback=attacker) -> onBuy calls setConsumed(group, type(uint256).max, maker) to cancel the maker's remaining offer capacity, violating the invariant that offer cancellation must not retroactively affect already-processed fills, causing scoped impact: buyer fills an offer and then cancels the maker's remaining capacity in the same callback, preventing the maker from filling other offers in the same group? Proof idea: assert that setConsumed called during onBuy with amount > consumed[maker][group] succeeds and sets consumed to type(uint256).max, and verify this does not affect the current fill.

[File: 'src/interfaces/ICallbacks.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: multicall / take (buy) + withdraw (insufficient withdrawable)] Can an unprivileged lender under preconditions where the lender has credit but withdrawable < credit, trigger the multicall sequence [take(sellOffer, units, lender, ...) to increase credit, withdraw(market, units, lender, receiver)] in one transaction, violating the invariant that withdraw requires withdrawable >= units and that credit increases from take do not increase withdrawable, causing scoped impact: lender attempts to withdraw newly acquired credit units that are not yet backed by withdrawable loan tokens, revealing whether the withdrawable check correctly prevents premature withdrawal of credit that has no corresponding token backing? Proof idea: assert that after take(sellOffer), marketState[id].withdrawable is unchanged, and that withdraw reverts if withdrawable < units.

[File: 'src/interfaces/ICallbacks.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: IRepayCallback.onRepay / repay + take (debt re-creation)] Can an unprivileged borrower-as-callback under preconditions where the borrower has debt and the repay callback fires after debt is decremented, trigger the sequence repay(market, units, borrower, callback, data) -> onRepay calls take(sellOffer, units, borrower, receiver, takerCallback=address(0)) to re-create the same debt, violating the invariant that repay must result in a net debt decrease, causing scoped impact: borrower repays debt and immediately re-borrows in the same callback, with the net effect that debt is unchanged but the borrower received loan tokens from the sell, effectively extracting loan tokens without net debt reduction? Proof idea: assert position[id][borrower].debt after the full repay call equals debt_before - units + sellerDebtIncrease_from_nested_take.

[File: 'src/interfaces/ICallbacks.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: multicall / flashLoan + take (sell) + repay (zero-capital borrow)] Can an unprivileged borrower under preconditions where the borrower has collateral but no loan tokens, trigger the multicall sequence [flashLoan([loanToken], [units], callback, data)] where onFlashLoan calls take(sellOffer, units, borrower, callback, takerCallback=address(0)) to borrow units, then repays the flash loan from the received sellerAssets, violating the invariant that a borrower must have net capital to fund a borrow, causing scoped impact: borrower creates a debt position with zero upfront capital by using the protocol's flash loan to fund the take's payer requirement, with the net effect that the borrower has debt and the protocol's balance is unchanged? Proof idea: assert borrower's loanToken balance before and after the multicall is unchanged, and position[id][borrower].debt == units.

[File: 'src/interfaces/ICallbacks.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: IFlashLoanCallback.onFlashLoan / flashLoan + take (buy) + withdraw (credit arbitrage)] Can an unprivileged lender under preconditions where the protocol has existing withdrawable loan tokens, trigger the sequence flashLoan([loanToken], [buyerAssets], callback, data) -> onFlashLoan calls take(sellOffer, units, lender, receiver, takerCallback=address(0)) to buy credit, then calls withdraw(market, units, lender, callback) to withdraw the credit, then repays the flash loan from the withdrawn tokens, violating the invariant that credit acquired via take is not immediately withdrawable (withdrawable is not increased by take), causing scoped impact: lender attempts to acquire credit and immediately withdraw it in the same flash loan callback, revealing whether the withdrawable check prevents this? Proof idea: assert that after take(sellOffer), marketState[id].withdrawable is unchanged, and withdraw reverts if withdrawable < units.

[File: 'src/interfaces/ICallbacks.sol -> Scope: Critical Multicall or sequencing bug that breaks invariants only when multiple protocol actions are composed in one transaction'] [Function: multicall / take (sell) + take (buy) (debt-credit netting)] Can an unprivileged user under preconditions where the user has no existing position, trigger the multicall sequence [take(sellOffer, units, user, ...) to create debt, take(buyOffer, units, user, ...) to create credit] in one transaction, violating the invariant that buying units must reduce existing debt before increasing credit (line 382: buyerCreditIncrease = zeroFloorSub(units, buyerPos.debt)), causing scoped impact: user creates debt in the first take and then the second take correctly nets the debt, but the health check after the first take must pass with the debt before the second take's credit offset, potentially allowing a position that is temporarily unhealthy between the two takes? Proof idea: assert isHealthy(market, id, user) after the first take completes, before the second take begins.
